title: MCA Reminds Companies: Form DPT-3 Filing Deadline is June 30, 2026!
content:
# MCA Reminds Companies: Form DPT-3 Filing Deadline is June 30, 2026!

Ek important update hai. Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) ne recently companies ko Form DPT-3 timely file karne ke liye remind kiya hai. This advisory, issued around June 11, 2026, emphasizes the approaching deadline for the annual return.

Here's a quick rundown of what you need to know:

*   **What is it?** Form DPT-3 is an annual return of deposits and/or outstanding receipts of money or loans not considered as deposits. It covers all such amounts outstanding as on March 31, 2026.
*   **Who needs to file?** Every company, except a government company, that has outstanding loans, deposits, or any receipts of money not falling under the definition of deposits, must file this form. This includes director loans, inter-company loans, and customer advances. Even if there are no such amounts, filing a NIL return is often recommended as a best practice.
*   **Why is it important?** Filing DPT-3 ensures financial transparency and compliance with Rule 16 of the Companies (Acceptance of Deposits) Rules, 2014. Non-compliance can lead to significant penalties under the Companies Act, 2013, including hefty fines for both the company and its officers.
*   **When is the deadline?** The last date for filing Form DPT-3 for the financial year 2025-26 is **June 30, 2026**.

Companies are advised to complete their filing well before the due date to avoid any last-minute issues and ensure regulatory compliance.

## Read the Official Document
* [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.mca.gov.in)

*Published on: 13 June 2026*
